title: Woman, 69, killed in Swampscott fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 69-year-old woman died after a fire in her Swampscott apartment on Thursday, according to the district attorney.
The fire occurred at a condominium complex on Loring Avenue; the woman was found in her apartment and later died.
State Police, Swampscott Police and Fire Department, and State Fire Marshal are investigating the cause of the fire.

title: In need of a top center, Bruins should dive into Dylan Larkin sweepstakes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin has requested a trade, drawing interest from teams like the Boston Bruins.
Larkin, a consistent 30-goal scorer, would address the Bruins' need for a top center to boost their lineup.
Larkin's no-trade clause and current contract make him a key asset but could require significant trade offers.

title: 2 of the best boat tours in America are in Maine, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Acadian Boat Tours in Bar Harbor ranked No. 8 in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards.
Sea Princess Cruises in Northeast Harbor placed No. 10 for best boat tours in the U.S. according to USA Today readers.
Both tours offer scenic views around Acadia National Park and include wildlife and sunset cruises.

title: As the World Cup comes to Foxborough, homeowners are renting out their lawns for parking
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Residents near Gillette Stadium are renting yards for parking during the 2026 World Cup, facing high demand.
Foxborough bans residential parking lot use, but nearby towns like Walpole issue permits to homeowners.
Parking on residential properties offers cheaper alternatives to the $175 stadium parking, attracting spontaneous visitors.

title: Hopkinton police seeking information after ‘suspicious video’ of person walking into roadway at night circulates online
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Hopkinton police are investigating a suspicious video of a person walking into a roadway at night.
The person in the video wore a construction vest, helmet, and Halloween mask, and carried a shovel.
Police urge anyone with information about the incident to contact them at 508-497-3401.

title: Boston City Council approves $1.7 billion schools budget that cuts hundreds of jobs
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council approved a $1.73 billion Boston Public Schools budget, eliminating over 400 student-facing jobs.
The budget, passed 8-5, follows declining enrollment and rising healthcare costs as reasons for the job cuts.
Protests from educators and Boston Teachers Union urge reconsideration, citing impact on vulnerable students.

title: Boozy ice cream is coming to Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: New York-based Tipsy Scoop will open a South End Boston location at 59 Dartmouth St. next spring.
Tipsy Scoop will offer catering and delivery of their alcohol-infused ice cream and sorbet before storefront opens.
Kimberly Medina, with strong Boston ties, owns the new franchise; non-alcoholic options also available.

title: Kevin McHale says Larry Bird would light up the modern NBA like a ‘five-alarm fire’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale believes Larry Bird would excel in the modern NBA, comparing his potential impact to a 'five-alarm fire'.
McHale argued Bird's physical attributes surpass those of current star Luka Doncic, who led the league in scoring last season.
Bird, a three-time NBA MVP, excelled in shooting, rebounding, and defense, winning three titles and multiple All-Star selections.

title: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in the shooting of Stephenson King Jr. in Roxbury.
Prosecutors allege O'Malley used excessive force during the incident; his defense claims he acted appropriately under the circumstances.
O'Malley, released on personal recognizance, is required to surrender firearms and faces a court date on July 23.

title: Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Embrace Boston acquired a 35,000-square-foot property on West St. in Downtown Crossing for a new civic center.
The space will include rotating art exhibitions, a media production area, and community gathering places.
The project is supported by donations and financing, with plans to open by Boston's 400th birthday in 2030.
